word_list.word: needs must when the devil drives
word_list.pronunciation: nňąiňźdz mňą Ćst w…õn √į…ô dňą…õv…ôl d…Ļňąa…™vz

senses.definition: used to imply that in desperate times, one must take drastic action regardless of how difficult or unpleasant it may be

examples.x: Needs must when the devil drives, so against my will I accepted the unpleasant job to pay the bills.

word_list.word: a drowning man will clutch at a straw
word_list.pronunciation: …ź d…Ļňąa än…™Ňč mňą√¶n w…™l klňą Ćt É √¶…ĺ…ô st…Ļňą…Ēňź

senses.definition: used to suggest that when people are facing a crisis or are in danger, they will try anything to save themselves, even if their efforts are unlikely to succeed

examples.x: Acknowledging that a drowning man will clutch at a straw, the defendant grasped at the flimsy alibi even though it appeared implausible, hoping it might provide some reprieve from the charges against him.

word_list.word: a hungry wolf is fixed to no place
word_list.pronunciation: …ź hňą ĆŇč…°…Ļi wňą älf …™z fňą…™kst t…ô nňąo ä plňąe…™s

senses.definition: used for saying that a desperate person will constantly seek opportunities and resources wherever they can find them in order to meet their needs

examples.x: My ragged friend explained that a hungry wolf is fixed to no place, apologizing for not staying in touch but saying he drifted wherever he could find work or means of shelter.

senses.definition: used to imply that in a difficult or dangerous situation, one is willing to accept any help or take any refuge that is available, even if it is not ideal

examples.x: When her car broke down in the middle of nowhere, she accepted a ride from a stranger, thinking, "Any port in a storm."
Faced with a tight deadline and limited resources, the team decided to use an outdated software program - it was a case of "any port in a storm."
The hikers, caught in a sudden downpour, took shelter in a small cabin they stumbled upon, realizing that "any port in a storm" was better than getting drenched.
During the power outage, people flocked to the nearest open restaurant, adhering to the principle of "any port in a storm" to find warm food and a place to sit.
In the midst of the pandemic, many businesses had to adapt quickly and embrace online sales, realizing that it was "any port in a storm" to survive the economic challenges.

word_list.pronunciation: hňą ĆŇč…°…ö b…Ļňąe…™ks stňąo än wňą…Ēňźlz

senses.definition: used to imply that when someone is extremely hungry or desperate, they will find a way to satisfy their needs even if it seems impossible

examples.x: When supplies ran low during the mountaineering expedition, the climbers realized that hunger breaks stone walls and undertook increasingly dangerous descents to forage for food.

word_list.pronunciation: skňą…Ēňź…Ļnf…ôl hňą ĆŇč…°…Ļi dňą…Ďňź…°z w…™l ňąiňźt dňą…úňź…ĺi pňą äd…™Ňčz

senses.definition: used to imply that when people are facing an emergency or a crisis situation, they are willing to do things they would normally consider beneath them or that go against their usual values or beliefs

word_list.word: need makes the old wife trot
word_list.pronunciation: nňąiňźd mňĆe…™ks √į…™ ňąo äld wňąa…™f t…Ļňą…Ďňźt

senses.definition: used to suggest that when people have an important and urgent need, regardless of their age or other limitations, they can find a way to overcome those limitations and work hard to meet that need
